message.content:
Fetch the actual source code from the oblivious_node repository that defines the RPC interface. I need to see:

1. The RPC handler code - fetch from: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/src/rpc.rs
2. The main oblivious node implementation: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/src/oblivious_node.rs
3. The lib.rs or main.rs entry point: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/src/lib.rs and https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/src/main.rs
4. Any test files that show usage patterns: check https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/tests/ 
5. The Cargo.toml for dependencies: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/obliviouslabs/oblivious_node/main/crates/eth_privatestate/Cargo.toml

Use WebFetch to get each file. For each file, extract the complete source code content. I need to understand:
- The exact JSON-RPC method signatures (params, return types)
- The data formats (hex encoding, RLP, etc.)
- The server configuration (host, port, etc.)
- How eth_getProof requests/responses are structured
- How admin_put_node and admin_set_root work
- Any WebSocket support

Return ALL source code you find, with full details.

content.text: I'll fetch all the source files in parallel to get the complete picture of the oblivious_node RPC interface.

content.content:
# Complete Cargo.toml File Content

Here is the complete content of the `eth_privatestate` Cargo.toml file:

```toml
[package]
name = "eth_privatestate"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false

[lints]
workspace = true

[dependencies]
tokio.workspace = true
jsonrpsee.workspace = true
serde.workspace = true
serde_json.workspace = true

trie-db.workspace = true
memory-db.workspace = true
rlp.workspace = true
hash-db.workspace = true
sha3.workspace = true
hex.workspace = true
rostl-datastructures.workspace = true
rostl-primitives.workspace = true
rostl-sort.workspace = true
rostl-oram.workspace = true
bytemuck.workspace = true

# Utils
anyhow.workspace = true
log.workspace = true
env_logger.workspace = true

[dev-dependencies]
reqwest.workspace = true
bytes.workspace = true
```

This Rust project uses workspace-managed dependencies across multiple categories: async runtime, JSON-RPC, serialization, cryptographic primitives, and data structures. All dependencies reference workspace versions rather than specifying individual version numbers.
